title: Refactor export-tasks: agent conversion, tier downgrades, and code extraction

content.title: Refactor export-tasks: agent conversion, tier downgrades, and code extraction
content.summary: A grill session reviewing the dispatch-tasks PR uncovered seven structural issues in export-tasks and related config. All were resolved: export-tasks converts to a true agent (agents/export-tasks.md), its description becomes routing-config-driven, both dispatch-tasks and export-tasks drop from T3 to T2, all bash operations that can be codified must be codified per the user's stated principle, a scriptable-code rule is added to the repo CLAUDE.md, the corporate wall label is retained, and an _active_corporate_domain doc field is added to task-routing.json.
decisions: Convert export-tasks from a skill (skills/export-tasks/SKILL.md) to a true agent (agents/export-tasks.md): drop the effort and spawn_mode frontmatter fields, add a tools list, keep model: sonnet.
The export-tasks description becomes destination-agnostic: remove the 'personal Notion' reference and describe routing to configured destinations via task-routing.json instead.
export-tasks drops from T3 to T2 in model-tiers.json. The work is mechanical (fixed 8-step script, API calls, file writeback) with no deep reasoning required.
dispatch-tasks drops from T3 to T2 in model-tiers.json. Routing logic is fully specified in task-routing.json; no synthesis or context-awareness required beyond following the config.

Per the user's stated principle 'if something can be written in code, it should be written in code': all bash operations in export-tasks that are mechanical and scriptable (token sourcing check, GitHub issue creation, Notion REST call) must be codified as proper scripts in scripts/ with real argument handling. No bash operations remain as prose or inline templates.
Add a rule to the repo CLAUDE.md (/Users/eric/.dotfiles/.claude/CLAUDE.md): if something is mechanical and scriptable, it lives in code. This prevents interpretation drift.
Keep the corporate wall tier label in task-routing.json. It is concrete and unambiguous.

Add an _active_corporate_domain doc field to task-routing.json pointing to the current corporate domain (standard-metrics). This is a human pointer only, not read by code, updated whenever the active corporate domain changes.
content.next_action: Run /to-tasks to generate the implementation task breakdown
content.problem_statement: The dispatch-tasks PR added export-tasks as a skill with spawn_mode: agent, but the repo's agent mechanism resolves subagent_type via the agents/ directory. export-tasks also hardcodes scriptable operations as prose, carries an overly-specific description, and has inflated tier assignments.
content.solution: Convert export-tasks to a proper agent in agents/, update its description and tiers, codify all scriptable bash operations as scripts in scripts/, add a scriptable-code rule to the repo CLAUDE.md, and add an _active_corporate_domain pointer to task-routing.json.
implementation_decisions: Agent frontmatter fields: name, description, tools (list), model. No effort field. No spawn_mode field.
Extracted scripts land in claude-code-shared/scripts/ with real argument handling (no placeholder values inline).
The export-tasks skill-to-agent conversion and any dispatch-tasks reference updates must land in the same commit since Agent(subagent_type='export-tasks') only resolves if the agent is registered.
_active_corporate_domain is a top-level doc field in task-routing.json, not an operational field and not read by any code.
risks_and_tradeoffs: dispatch-tasks calls Agent(subagent_type='export-tasks'), which only resolves if export-tasks is registered as an agent. The skill-to-agent conversion and any dispatch-tasks reference updates must land together.
further_notes: spawn_mode: agent in the export-tasks frontmatter is the only use of that field across the entire repo. It is removed as part of the agent conversion with no other changes needed to support it.

tasks.title: Convert export-tasks skill to agent and update tiers
tasks.description: Create `claude-code-shared/agents/export-tasks.md` as a proper agent file (frontmatter: name, description, tools, model: sonnet — no effort field, no spawn_mode field). The description must be destination-agnostic: remove the 'personal Notion' reference, describe routing to configured destinations via task-routing.json instead. Copy the full process logic from the existing `skills/export-tasks/SKILL.md` into the new agent file. Remove `claude-code-shared/skills/export-tasks/SKILL.md` and its directory. Add export-tasks to `agents/registry.json` with model: sonnet and consumers: ["dispatch-tasks"]. Update `resources/model-tiers.json`: remove `"export-tasks": "T3"` from the skills section, add `"export-tasks": "T2"` to the agents section, and change `"dispatch-tasks": "T3"` to `"dispatch-tasks": "T2"` in the skills section. Run `python ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/sync-model-tiers.py --apply` to propagate the dispatch-tasks tier change to its skill frontmatter.
acceptance_criteria: `agents/export-tasks.md` exists with name: export-tasks, model: sonnet, and no effort or spawn_mode fields in frontmatter
`agents/export-tasks.md` description contains no reference to 'personal Notion'
`agents/registry.json` contains an entry for export-tasks with model: sonnet and consumers: [dispatch-tasks]
`model-tiers.json` agents section has `export-tasks: T2`; skills section has `dispatch-tasks: T2`
`skills/dispatch-tasks/SKILL.md` frontmatter shows `effort: medium` after sync-model-tiers runs

tasks.title: Extract scriptable bash operations from export-tasks into scripts
tasks.description: Create three scripts in `claude-code-shared/scripts/`: (1) `export-tasks-check-notion-token.sh` — sources `~/.dotfiles/local/secrets.env` if present, exits 0 if NOTION_PERSONAL_TOKEN is set, exits 1 with a descriptive error message if not; (2) `export-tasks-gh-issue.sh` — accepts positional args `<title> <body> <org/repo>`, validates all three are non-empty, runs `gh issue create --title "$1" --body "$2" --label triage --repo "$3"`, and prints the returned issue URL to stdout; (3) `export-tasks-notion-page-api.sh` — accepts positional args `<db_id> <title> <description>`, sources secrets.env, runs the Notion REST API curl call with NOTION_PERSONAL_TOKEN, and prints the returned page URL to stdout on success or a non-zero exit on failure. All three scripts must use `set -euo pipefail` and include argument count validation. Update `agents/export-tasks.md` to call these scripts by absolute path (`~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/<name>.sh`) in place of the inline prose/template blocks for those operations.
acceptance_criteria: `scripts/export-tasks-check-notion-token.sh` exits 1 with a descriptive message when NOTION_PERSONAL_TOKEN is unset
`scripts/export-tasks-gh-issue.sh` exits non-zero with a usage message when called with fewer than 3 arguments
`scripts/export-tasks-notion-page-api.sh` exits non-zero with a usage message when called with fewer than 3 arguments
`agents/export-tasks.md` step 2 token check calls `export-tasks-check-notion-token.sh` instead of inline bash
`agents/export-tasks.md` step 6 GitHub adapter calls `export-tasks-gh-issue.sh` instead of inline prose template
`agents/export-tasks.md` step 6 Notion REST API adapter calls `export-tasks-notion-page-api.sh` instead of inline curl template

tasks.title: Add scriptable-code rule to repo CLAUDE.md and _active_corporate_domain to task-routing.json
tasks.description: Edit `/Users/eric/.dotfiles/.claude/CLAUDE.md` to add a new section stating the scriptable-code principle: if something is mechanical and scriptable, it lives in code — not inline prose or template comments in skill/agent files. Edit `claude-code-shared/resources/task-routing.json` to add `"_active_corporate_domain": "standard-metrics"` as a top-level doc field adjacent to `_doc`. This field is a human pointer only — it is not read by any code and must be updated manually whenever the active corporate domain changes.
acceptance_criteria: `/Users/eric/.dotfiles/.claude/CLAUDE.md` contains a section with the scriptable-code rule
